mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already extremely low, the discount-rate channel dominates and equity valuations re-rate upward. Lower policy rates reduce financing costs and support risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month while the easing path is intact.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average during months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the signal’s rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above an already elevated level signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and higher recession odds. That combination should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal remains negative in recession-warning periods but forward returns are not below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand strength in consumer spending and housing activity. That combination should lift cyclical earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive co-movement in RSAFS and HOUST does not improve 20-day S&P 500 returns relative to either series alone, or if interaction terms fail to outperform a single-variable benchmark over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial output and tighter effective supply conditions for corporate revenue growth. When production momentum is negative, equity earnings revisions should worsen and the S&P 500 should underperform over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not negative after two straight monthly declines in INDPRO, or if the hit rate in such episodes does not exceed 55% over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a sharp FEDFUNDS increase transmits through higher discount rates and credit conditions with a lag. That effect should dominate short-horizon equity pricing and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon S&P 500 returns are positive in most episodes after a >0.25 pp two-month FEDFUNDS rise, or if the signal’s 5-day Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth weakens while UNRATE rises, the labor market is losing jobs and consumption support at the same time. This joint deterioration should forecast negative S&P 500 returns over the next month because earnings and demand expectations both deteriorate.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-signal does not outperform either PAYEMS or UNRATE alone in predicting 20-day returns, or if negative returns fail to occur in at least 55% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in GDPC1 relative to the prior quarter reflects stronger aggregate demand and better corporate revenue prospects. That macro impulse should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, especially when GDP acceleration is broad rather than isolated.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises from the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth does not precede positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a recovery regime, rising HOUST together with stable or falling UNRATE marks a durable demand rebound that improves cyclicals and market breadth. The S&P 500 should advance over the next quarter when housing starts strengthen without concurrent labor-market damage.
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST with non-rising UNRATE does not produce higher 60-day returns than other states, or if the interaction term loses significance in more than one consecutive rolling 3-year window.
